metadata.context: CPU implementation done (PR #745). Now need GPU validation with INSTALL_MAMBA=1 Docker image on RunPod RTX 4090. This plan covers the full end-to-end: Docker build, SkyPilot YAML creation, smoke test execution, VRAM measurement, and #744 closure.

phase.name: Pre-Flight Checks
phase.description: Verify all prerequisites before building the Docker image or provisioning any cloud resources. Every check must pass. No "probably fine" — run the commands. Matches Ralph Loop Step 1 (PRE-FLIGHT CHECK).

task.name: Verify cloud credentials and GPU availability
task.description: RunPod must be enabled in SkyPilot and RTX 4090 must be available. RunPod spot is often sold out (2026-03-14 metalearning) — on-demand is default.
task.acceptance: sky check runpod: ENABLED. At least one RTX4090/RTX5090/RTX3090 available.
task.failure-action: If sky check fails: verify RUNPOD_API_KEY in .env. If no GPUs: fall back to RTX 5090 or RTX 3090 (all ≥24 GB Ada, BF16 ✓). RTX 5090 preferred if available (newer Ada Lovelace, faster).

task.name: Verify MLflow server health (UpCloud)
task.description: UpCloud MLflow must be reachable before training. If it's down, artifacts are lost. Fix Pulumi stack first — do not proceed with downed MLflow.

commands.cmd: source .env && curl -s -u "${MLFLOW_CLOUD_USERNAME}:${MLFLOW_CLOUD_PASSWORD}" \ "${MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI}/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search" | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'MLflow OK: {len(d[\"experiments\"])} experiments')"

task.acceptance: MLflow OK: N experiments (N >= 0). Any error = BLOCKED. Fix UpCloud stack first.

task.name: Verify DVC data is pushed to UpCloud S3
task.description: DVC pull in the RunPod setup stage needs data in UpCloud S3. The md5 hash in smoke_test_mamba.yaml setup must match what's in the bucket.
task.acceptance: "Data and pipelines are up to date." If stale: `dvc push -r upcloud` first.

task.name: Verify .env has all required variables
task.description: The mamba smoke test YAML needs the same env vars as existing smoke tests (DVC_S3_*, MLFLOW_CLOUD_*, RUNPOD_API_KEY) plus no HF_TOKEN needed (mambavesselnet has no pretrained weights to download).

commands.cmd: python3 -c " from pathlib import Path required = ['DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL','DVC_S3_ACCESS_KEY','DVC_S3_SECRET_KEY', 'DVC_S3_BUCKET','MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI','MLFLOW_CLOUD_USERNAME', 'MLFLOW_CLOUD_PASSWORD','RUNPOD_API_KEY','GITHUB_TOKEN'] env = {} for line in Path('.env').read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines(): if '=' in line and not line.startswith('#'): k, _, v = line.partition('=') env[k.strip()] = v.strip() missing = [k for k in required if not env.get(k)] print('Missing:', missing or 'NONE — all set') "

task.acceptance: Missing: NONE — all set. If any missing: populate .env before proceeding.

task.name: Verify smoke_mambavesselnet_cloud.yaml has correct AMP policy
task.description: D04 AMP policy: train=ON, val=OFF. Confirm the experiment config has mixed_precision_val=false. This is a READ-ONLY check — do not modify.

commands.cmd: python3 -c " import yaml from pathlib import Path cfg = yaml.safe_load(Path('configs/experiment/smoke_mambavesselnet_cloud.yaml').read_text(encoding='utf-8')) amp_train = cfg.get('mixed_precision', 'MISSING') amp_val = cfg.get('mixed_precision_val', 'MISSING') print(f'mixed_precision={amp_train} (want True), mixed_precision_val={amp_val} (want False)') assert amp_train is True and amp_val is False, 'AMP policy violation!' print('AMP policy OK') "

task.acceptance: mixed_precision=True, mixed_precision_val=False. AMP policy OK. If assertion fails: DO NOT proceed. Fix the config YAML first.

phase.name: Build + Push :mamba-latest Docker Image
phase.description:
Build Dockerfile.base with INSTALL_MAMBA=1, which compiles mamba-ssm==2.3.1 CUDA extensions (Phase C in Dockerfile.base). Tag as :mamba-latest to keep the standard :latest image unchanged for labs without Mamba needs. Push to GHCR so RunPod SkyPilot can pull it.
Requires: nvcc (CUDA toolkit) in the Docker builder stage — already present in nvidia/cuda:12.6.3-devel-ubuntu24.04 (builder stage in Dockerfile.base). Mamba-ssm compile time: ~15–20 min (JIT compilation of selective scan CUDA kernels).

task.name: Add build-mamba Makefile target
task.description: Add `make build-mamba` and `make push-mamba` targets to the Makefile. Pattern follows existing `make build` and `make push-ghcr` targets. Tag: ${DOCKER_REGISTRY:-ghcr.io/petteriteikari}/minivess-base:mamba-latest
task.tdd-spec: RED: test that `make build-mamba --dry-run` resolves to docker build with INSTALL_MAMBA=1 and the :mamba-latest tag (Makefile dry-run check). GREEN: add build-mamba + push-mamba targets to Makefile. VERIFY: make test-staging passes, Makefile lint passes.
task.acceptance: `make build-mamba` target exists with INSTALL_MAMBA=1 build arg. `make push-mamba` target exists pointing to :mamba-latest tag. Both targets print usage hint if DOCKER_REGISTRY not set.

task.name: Build minivess-base:mamba-latest
task.description:
Run docker build with INSTALL_MAMBA=1. This triggers Phase C in Dockerfile.base: `uv sync --frozen --no-dev --extra mamba-ssm` which pip-installs mamba-ssm from source and compiles the CUDA kernels with nvcc.
Expected build time: 30–45 min (CUDA kernel compilation dominates). Expected image size: +2–4 GB over :latest (CUDA compiled objects).

commands.cmd: DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build \ --build-arg INSTALL_MAMBA=1 \ --build-arg UID=$(id -u) \ --build-arg GID=$(id -g) \ -t ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base \ . 2>&1 | tee /tmp/docker-build-mamba.log

task.acceptance: Build exits 0. No "CUDA compilation error" in log. Image exists locally. Verify mamba-ssm installed: `docker run --rm ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest python -c "import mamba_ssm; print(mamba_ssm.__version__)"` Should print: 2.3.1 (or similar). Must NOT raise ImportError.
task.failure-action: MAMBA_COMPILE_FAIL: Check /tmp/docker-build-mamba.log for nvcc errors. Common cause: CUDA version mismatch between PyTorch and nvcc. Fix: check torch.version.cuda matches CUDA driver version in base image. Self-learning TDD: If compile fails due to Python/config issue (not hardware), trigger self-learning-iterative-coder to write a failing test and fix.

task.name: Push :mamba-latest to GHCR
task.description: Push the built image to GHCR. RunPod SkyPilot pulls from GHCR during job launch. Requires GITHUB_TOKEN with packages:write scope in .env.

commands.cmd: docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest | python3 -c "import json,sys; m=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'GHCR push OK: {m[\"schemaVersion\"]=}, layers={len(m[\"layers\"])}')"

task.acceptance: docker manifest inspect returns valid manifest with multiple layers. Timestamp on GHCR is within last 2 hours.

phase.name: SkyPilot YAML + Makefile Target
phase.description:
Create deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml for RunPod MambaVesselNet smoke test. Pattern: extends smoke_test_gpu.yaml but uses :mamba-latest image, sets MODEL_FAMILY=mambavesselnet, and adds mamba-ssm verification in setup. Add make smoke-test-mamba target.
Key differences from smoke_test_gpu.yaml: - image_id: docker:ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest - MODEL_FAMILY: mambavesselnet (not sam3_vanilla) - No HF_TOKEN needed (no pretrained weights) - Setup: python -c "import mamba_ssm" verification step - Setup: VRAM baseline print before training - Run: EXPERIMENT=smoke_mambavesselnet (uses smoke_mambavesselnet_cloud.yaml)

task.name: Create deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml
task.description: New SkyPilot YAML for MambaVesselNet smoke test on RunPod. Must follow all SkyPilot + Docker mandates from CLAUDE.md: - image_id: docker:ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest - No apt-get/uv sync/git clone in setup (all deps in image) - Setup is DATA ONLY (DVC pull + splits copy) - MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 + PREFECT_DISABLED=1 (dev environment) - sky jobs launch (NOT sky launch) for spot recovery VRAM capture: after training, print VRAM via torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() to satisfy T09 VRAM measurement requirement.
task.template: Base on: deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml Change image_id: docker:ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest Change accelerators: {RTX4090: 1, RTX5090: 1, RTX3090: 1} (same — all ≥24 GB) Remove HF_TOKEN env var (not needed for mambavesselnet) Add to setup after nvidia-smi check: python -c "import mamba_ssm; print(f'mamba_ssm {mamba_ssm.__version__} CUDA ready')" Change run: EXPERIMENT=smoke_mambavesselnet Add VRAM capture in run (after training exits 0): python -c " import torch if torch.cuda.is_available(): vram_mb = torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() // (1024*1024) print(f'VRAM_PEAK_MB={vram_mb}') # T09: captured by Ralph monitor "
task.acceptance: File exists at deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml. `sky validate deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml` passes (if sky validate exists). image_id references :mamba-latest, MODEL_FAMILY=mambavesselnet. No apt-get, uv sync, git clone in setup block.

task.name: Add make smoke-test-mamba target
task.description: Add Makefile targets consistent with existing smoke-test-gpu pattern: make smoke-test-mamba — launch smoke test (on-demand, reliable) make smoke-test-mamba-spot — spot instance (cheaper, may be preempted) make verify-smoke-test-mamba — verify MLflow artifacts after test make smoke-test-mamba-preflight — run P0 pre-flight checks only
task.acceptance: All four targets exist in Makefile. `make smoke-test-mamba --dry-run` shows `sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml`. Uses `--env-file .env` to load credentials. References `MODEL_FAMILY=mambavesselnet`.

phase.name: Launch + Monitor
phase.description: Execute the MambaVesselNet smoke test via Ralph Loop. Launch → Monitor (30s poll) → collect VRAM and training results. Target: smoke_mambavesselnet_cloud config (3 epochs, 1 fold, 4 volumes). Expected training time on RTX 4090: ~6–10 min. Expected val_loss: finite (no NaN — AMP val OFF per D04).

task.name: Launch smoke test via SkyPilot
task.description: MUST use `sky jobs launch` (not `sky launch`). This enables spot recovery. `sky launch` creates a cluster — no automatic preemption recovery. `sky jobs launch` treats the job as managed — SkyPilot recovers automatically. Cost estimate: $0.10 per 10 min on RunPod RTX 4090 spot ($0.60/hr after spot discount).

commands.cmd: sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml \ --env-file .env \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=mambavesselnet \ -y 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ralph-launch-mamba.log

task.acceptance: Job ID returned and written to /tmp/ralph-launch-mamba.log. `sky jobs status` shows job in PENDING or RUNNING state.
task.failure-action: GPU_SOLD_OUT: Try RTX5090 or RTX3090 (all on accelerators list). DOCKER_AUTH_FAIL: Re-run `make push-mamba` with fresh GITHUB_TOKEN. DOCKER_PULL_FAIL: Make GHCR package public (GitHub → Packages → minivess-base → Settings).

task.name: Monitor job to completion (30s poll)
task.description: Poll `sky jobs logs --sync` every 30s. Watch for: - SUCCEEDED: normal exit → go to Phase 5 - FAILED: go to Phase 4 (diagnose) - GPU_OOM: `CUDA out of memory` in logs → Phase 4 (OOM fix) - NaN: `val_loss=nan` in MLflow logs → Phase 4 (AMP debug) - MAMBA_COMPILE_FAIL: `mamba_ssm ImportError` → Phase 4 (Docker rebuild) Ralph Loop poll logic: use `sky jobs logs --sync 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt`
task.acceptance: Job exits with SUCCEEDED status. Logs contain: `mamba_ssm 2.3.1 CUDA ready` (Phase C compiled OK). Logs contain: `VRAM_PEAK_MB=NNNN` (T09 capture line). Logs contain: training loss sequence showing finite values.

phase.name: Diagnose + Fix
phase.description: Failure diagnosis and fix, following Ralph Loop Steps 4–5. Known mambavesselnet-specific failure patterns listed below. For code-level failures, delegate to self-learning-iterative-coder (TDD RED→GREEN→FIX). For infrastructure failures, fix config/env and relaunch directly. Max 3 retries. After 3: escalate to user with diagnosis report.

pattern.description: mamba_ssm CUDA extension can't find libcuda.so at runtime. Happens when the runtime image lacks CUDA runtime libs (builder vs runner image mismatch).
pattern.fix: Verify Dockerfile.base runner stage copies mamba-ssm .so files along with .venv. Check: `docker run --rm --gpus all minivess-base:mamba-latest python -c "import mamba_ssm"`. If fails locally: the runner stage is missing CUDA runtime libs. Add `libcuda.so` to runner stage COPY or switch runner to cuda:12.6.3-cudnn-runtime. TDD: write test_mamba_runtime_import.py in tests/gpu_instance/, fix Dockerfile.

pattern.description: mamba-ssm CUDA kernels compiled against a different CUDA version than the runtime driver. Build: nvidia/cuda:12.6.3-devel. Runtime: nvidia/cuda:12.6.3-runtime.
pattern.fix: Ensure builder and runner base images use identical CUDA version tag. Check ARG CUDA_VERSION in Dockerfile.base; must match between builder/runner.

pattern.description: MambaVesselNet bottleneck dim=768 with bidirectional SSM. Estimated VRAM: ~5–8 GB for patch_size=(64,64,64), batch_size=1. RTX 4090 (24 GB) should not OOM. If OOM: likely patch_size too large or batch_size > 1.
pattern.fix: Step 1: Check smoke_mambavesselnet_cloud.yaml for batch_size and patch_size. Step 2: If batch_size > 1, set batch_size: 1 and relaunch. Step 3: If patch_size is (96,96,96), reduce to (64,64,64) (D05 eval_roi_size). Step 4: Check VRAM log from previous attempt: VRAM_PEAK_MB=NNNN. Auto-fix: update config YAML and relaunch (no code change needed). If still OOM at (64,64,64) batch=1: escalate — unexpected, needs investigation.

pattern.description: NaN in validation loss. D04 AMP policy: val OFF (mixed_precision_val=false). If NaN: AMP policy violated — autocast is active during validation. Verify: smoke_mambavesselnet_cloud.yaml has mixed_precision_val=false.
pattern.fix: Step 1: Check logs for `AMP: validation ON` message — should say OFF. Step 2: Verify configs/experiment/smoke_mambavesselnet_cloud.yaml. Step 3: If config correct, check train_flow.py applies val amp policy correctly. TDD: write test for val AMP policy, fix train_flow.py. Ref: MONAI #4243.

pattern.description: BidirectionalMambaLayer reshape error: (B,C,D,H,W) → (B,L,C) → (B,C,D,H,W). CPU tests pass (MockMamba), but real Mamba may expose edge cases. Divisor constraint: all spatial dims must be divisible by divisor=16.
pattern.fix: Step 1: Extract exact shapes from error message (NEVER regex — use str.partition). Step 2: Verify patch_size is divisible by 16 in all 3 dims (64/16=4 ✓). Step 3: If not: fix patch_size in smoke_mambavesselnet_cloud.yaml. Step 4: If patch is valid and reshape still fails: add TDD test for that shape in tests/v2/unit/test_mambavesselnet_blocks.py, fix BidirectionalMambaLayer. Self-learning TDD: RED (test for exact failing shape) → GREEN → CHECKPOINT → relaunch.

pattern.description: DVC pull failed silently. Training data missing in container.
pattern.fix: Verify dvc.lock md5 hash matches UpCloud S3 content. Run: `dvc push -r upcloud` locally to refresh. Then relaunch.

task.name: Classify failure and apply fix
task.description: Parse /tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt using str.partition() and `in` checks (no regex). Match against known-failure-patterns above + universal ralph-loop patterns. Classify as auto-fixable or needs-user. Write structured diagnosis to outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl. Apply fix and relaunch (max 3 attempts total across all retries).
task.self-learning-tdd-trigger: Trigger self-learning-iterative-coder skill when: - RESHAPE_ERROR and shape is valid → code bug in BidirectionalMambaLayer - MAMBA_IMPORT_FAIL and image correct → code bug in import guard - MAMBA_NAN_VAL and config correct → code bug in AMP policy application - Any failure not in known-failure-patterns → investigate, write test, fix TDD cycle: RED (write test reproducing cloud failure) → GREEN (fix code) → VERIFY (make test-staging) → CHECKPOINT (git commit) → relaunch cloud job.
task.acceptance: outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl has entry for each attempt. If auto-fixable: relaunched and completed. If needs-user: reported clearly.

phase.name: VRAM Record + Verification + Report
phase.description: Post-success: extract VRAM measurement, verify MLflow artifacts, update model profile YAML, close GitHub issue #744, write final report. Ralph Loop Step 6 (REPORT).

task.name: Extract VRAM measurement from logs
task.description: Extract VRAM_PEAK_MB from training logs (printed in run: block of YAML). This satisfies T09 (VRAM measurement on RTX 4090) from the implementation plan. Expected peak VRAM for MambaVesselNet++ patch=(64,64,64), batch=1: ~5–8 GB (bottleneck=768-dim, bidirMamba × 2 = 1536 channels, large but fits 24 GB). If VRAM > 20 GB (close to limit): document as "needs A100 for production".

commands.cmd: python3 -c " from pathlib import Path log = Path('/tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt').read_text(encoding='utf-8') for line in log.splitlines(): if 'VRAM_PEAK_MB' in line: key, _, val = line.partition('=') print(f'T09 VRAM measurement: {val.strip()} MB ({int(val.strip()) // 1024:.1f} GB)') break else: print('WARNING: VRAM_PEAK_MB not found in logs') "

task.acceptance: VRAM measurement printed with numeric value. Value > 0 and value < 24576 (24 GB RTX 4090 total).

task.name: Update model profile YAML with measured VRAM
task.description: Write measured VRAM to configs/model_profiles/mambavesselnet.yaml. Currently: `vram.measured: false` (T09 placeholder from T07). After measurement: update with actual values. Also update `notes` to reflect measured status.
task.tdd-spec: No new tests needed — existing test_mambavesselnet_configs.py validates the YAML structure. After updating, re-run test to confirm schema is still valid.
task.acceptance: configs/model_profiles/mambavesselnet.yaml: vram.measured: true vram.rtx4090_patch64: [measured value in MB] vram.notes: "Measured 2026-03-16 on RunPod RTX 4090 (24 GB)" make test-staging passes.

task.name: Verify MLflow artifacts on UpCloud
task.description: Verify the smoke test run is visible in UpCloud MLflow with expected metrics. Pattern from existing verify-smoke-test Makefile target.

commands.cmd: source .env && python3 -c " import mlflow, os mlflow.set_tracking_uri(os.environ['MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI']) client = mlflow.tracking.MlflowClient() expts = client.search_experiments(filter_string='name LIKE \"%mambavesselnet%\"') print(f'Found {len(expts)} mambavesselnet experiments') if expts: runs = client.search_runs(expts[0].experiment_id, order_by=['start_time DESC']) if runs: r = runs[0] val_loss = r.data.metrics.get('val_loss', 'MISSING') train_loss = r.data.metrics.get('train_loss', 'MISSING') print(f'Latest run: {r.info.run_id[:8]} status={r.info.status}') print(f' val_loss={val_loss}, train_loss={train_loss}') import math assert r.info.status == 'FINISHED', f'Run not FINISHED: {r.info.status}' assert isinstance(val_loss, float) and not math.isnan(val_loss), f'val_loss is NaN!' print('MLflow verification PASSED') "

task.acceptance: 1+ experiments with "mambavesselnet" in name. Latest run: status=FINISHED, val_loss finite (not NaN), not MISSING.

task.name: Commit VRAM update + close GitHub issue #744
task.description: Commit the updated configs/model_profiles/mambavesselnet.yaml with measured VRAM. Close GitHub issue #744 (T09 VRAM + T10 cloud smoke test) with verification summary. Reference the MLflow run ID in the close comment.

commands.cmd: git commit -m "feat(mambavesselnet): T09 — VRAM measured on RTX 4090, closes #744"

gh issue close 744 --comment "T09 VRAM measured + T10 smoke test PASSED on RunPod RTX 4090. See MLflow run [run_id] on UpCloud. VRAM peak: [NNNN] MB @ patch=(64,64,64), batch=1. AMP: train=ON, val=OFF (D04, MONAI #4243). val_loss=finite. Branch: test/mambavesselnet"

task.acceptance: Issue #744 closed on GitHub. Commit appears on test/mambavesselnet branch. All tdd-state.json T09+T10 statuses updated to DONE.

task.name: Update tdd-state.json and write final report
task.description: Update .claude/skills/self-learning-iterative-coder/state/tdd-state.json: T09.status: "DONE" (was: "blocked") T10.status: "DONE" (was: "blocked") convergence.reached: true (all 11 tasks DONE) Write summary to outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl (ralph loop report). Append to LEARNINGS.md: mamba-ssm CUDA build gotchas observed.
task.acceptance: tdd-state.json: T09+T10 DONE, convergence.tasks_done=11, tasks_blocked=0. outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl has final report entry with total cost and duration. LEARNINGS.md has a new entry for 2026-03-16 with any discoveries from the cloud run.

cost-model.spot-rate: ~$0.44/hr (often sold out — use on-demand for reliability)
cost-model.note: Smoke test: 3 epochs, 1 fold, 4 volumes (smoke_test_1fold_4vol.json). NOT scientifically valid — infrastructure + VRAM verification only. Production training (100 epochs, 3 folds) on GCP L4 is a separate issue.

ralph-loop-parameters.self-learning-tdd-trigger: Activate self-learning-iterative-coder when failure is CODE-LEVEL: - Pattern not in known-failure-patterns list - Infrastructure checks pass but Python code raises exception - Shape/type errors in model forward pass Do NOT activate for infrastructure failures (GPU_SOLD_OUT, DOCKER_AUTH_FAIL, DVC_MISSING).

close.reason: T09 VRAM measured on RTX 4090 + T10 smoke test PASSED on RunPod (Phase 5 complete)
